title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: Studio is an internal workspace that answers business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. It generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Three reliability techniques enable production use: preflight sequencing with just-in-time schema injection, explicit layering rules to force model reliance on primary sources over learned knowledge, and query validation before widget hardcoding.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS presents Studio, an internal workspace that answers business questions in natural language by querying Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. Rather than re-running model calls each time, Studio generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. The talk details three reliability techniques for production use: preflight sequencing with just-in-time schema injection, explicit layering rules that force the model to rely on primary sources over learned knowledge, and query validation before a widget is hardcoded. The takeaway is a pattern for turning natural language to SQL into stable, low-cost artifacts through context management and test-time control.
